title: mattiastrum polycarpum
intro: Mattiastrum polycarpum, also known as the desert holly or desert broom, is a flowering plant native to the deserts of southwestern North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Mattiastrum polycarpum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or externally to harness its medicinal benefits.
